page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the n4d family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent disk volumes.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core instances. However, the lack of local NVMe SSDs and integrated GPUs limits its utility for high-throughput storage operations or hardware-accelerated workloads. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density per core allows users to run memory-bound applications without paying for unnecessary vCPU capacity.

This hardware profile is designed for workloads that require a larger memory footprint relative to compute power. Suitable applications include small-scale databases, caching layers, in-memory data processing, and lightweight enterprise applications that do not demand intensive parallel processing or local disk performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory. Part of the n4d server family, it provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU, making it a memory-optimized option for workloads requiring significant RAM relative to compute resources. The instance features dedicated CPU allocation for predictable performance, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. This resource configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound tasks, as it avoids the need to provision excess vCPUs to obtain sufficient memory. The n4d-highmem-2 is suitable for small-scale databases, in-memory caching, and lightweight enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highmem-2 is an x86_64 server with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-bound workloads without local storage or GPUs.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core x86_64 server featuring sixteen gigabytes of memory for memory-intensive workloads requiring predictable compute performance.

No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Predictable performance via dedicated CPU allocation
Suited for small-scale databases and in-memory caching
